description: A plain black-and-white (sepia/duotone) historical studio portrait photograph of President Theodore Roosevelt, with no added on-image text, graphics, or design elements. Roosevelt is shown from roughly the knees up, standing in a three-piece dark suit with a patterned necktie and his characteristic pince-nez glasses and bushy mustache, looking sternly off to the side against a mottled cloudy studio backdrop. The image is a vintage archival photograph reposted as-is to accompany the tweet announcing his induction into the Department of Labor's Labor Hall of Honor. Because it carries no designed typography, slogans, seals or meme styling, it is a candid/archival photo rather than a designed graphic. Target audience: general public and history-minded supporters, used to lend gravitas to a DOL honor announcement.
